title: AMU Survey: Tightening aluminum market not met by inventory rebuilding
summary: This market update highlights a tightening aluminum market and notes that inventory rebuilding has not kept pace with supply-demand conditions. It suggests near-term pricing and market balance may remain sensitive to stock levels and broader demand trends.

title: Edward Meir's week in review and thoughts for the week of May 25, 2026
summary: This weekly commentary reviews recent aluminum market developments and discusses the outlook for the week of May 25, 2026. It likely focuses on price direction, market drivers, and supply-demand conditions affecting aluminum trading.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that aluminum fell to 3,627 USD/T on May 27, 2026, down 1.40% on the day. It also notes the price is up 2.47% over the past month and 46.81% over the past year, indicating a strong longer-term uptrend despite the daily drop.

title: Century Aluminum to restart production in South Carolina, citing tariffs
summary: Century Aluminum will restart more than 50,000 metric tons of idled production at its Mt. Holly, South Carolina smelter to strengthen domestic supply as Section 232 tariffs affect aluminum imports. The company said it will invest $50 million, create more than 100 jobs, and raise U.S. aluminum production by nearly 10%, with full production expected by June 30, 2026.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Fox News reports that Century Aluminum is increasing production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs. The segment frames the move as a major boost to U.S. aluminum output, but the provided result is a video clip rather than a full article.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process designed to reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production. The membrane captured more than 99% of aluminum ions in lab-scale tests, which could both cut waste and increase yield by recovering aluminum from effluent streams.

title: Decarbonizing Aluminum: Rolling Out a More Sustainable Sector
summary: This analysis explains how increased aluminum recycling and decarbonization technology have already lowered the carbon intensity of aluminum production. It also highlights Elysis, a joint venture involving Apple, Alcoa, Rio Tinto, Canada, and Quebec, which aims to eliminate direct greenhouse gas emissions from aluminum smelting.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: The article outlines several pathways for reducing emissions in aluminum production, including technologies that eliminate carbon emissions during electrolysis and improve recycled aluminum quality. It emphasizes that innovation in refining, recycling, and process design can support a lower-carbon aluminum industry.

title: Roadmap to net-zero
summary: Hydro says it is committed to reaching net-zero emissions in aluminium production by 2050 or earlier through cutting-edge technology. The roadmap is focused on sustainability improvements across the company’s aluminum operations.

title: HRC steel hits highest level since December 2023 as prices edge lower on the day
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel increased to 1,143 USD/T, the highest level since December 2023, while the same page also notes a 0.60% day-over-day decline to 1,152.05 USD/T on May 27. The article reflects a market that has been firm over recent weeks but still experiencing normal daily volatility.

title: Steel rises modestly in Chinese market, continuing recent gains
summary: Steel rose to 3,165 CNY/T on May 28, up 0.25% from the previous day. Trading Economics also reports that steel prices have gained over the past month and are higher versus the prior year, pointing to an overall upward trend.

title: Steel price news shows broad strength across flat, plate, and long products
summary: Ryerson says latest steel price news shows prices continuing to move higher across flat, plate, and long products. The company attributes the trend to constrained supply and improving demand supporting the market.

title: EU steel trade measures move toward entry into force
summary: Argus reports on an EU regulation that would set tariff-rate quotas of 18.3 million tonnes per year for steel and impose a 50% out-of-quota duty on 30 categories of imported steel products. The policy could affect market access and pricing dynamics in Europe once fully approved.

title: Steel sheet and plate price indices rise again
summary: Steel Market Update says sheet and plate price indices increased by $5 to $20 per short ton from the prior week. The update indicates continued firmness in the U.S. steel market heading into late May.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: This article says U.S. steel market indicators were stabilizing, with recent production levels running above the year-to-date average and mill capability utilization improving week over week. It also notes that longer-term global and domestic steel output forecasts turn positive in 2026, although tariffs and prior weakness had pressured the market.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reported that domestic raw steel production in the week ending May 23, 2026 totaled 1,870,000 net tons, with a capability utilization rate of 81.0 percent. This provides the latest weekly snapshot of U.S. steel output and operating capacity.

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: This report says U.S. steel manufacturers are planning more than $6 billion in capital-spending projects that are expected to begin in the near term. The story focuses on upcoming capacity additions and investment activity in the steel sector.

title: Community reacts to news of $2.7 billion dollar steel sheet mill
summary: This news segment covers reactions to Nucor's announced $2.7 billion steel sheet mill in Mason County. It says the facility is expected to produce 3 million tons of steel annually and bring new jobs and construction activity to the area.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights several recent advances in lower-carbon steelmaking, including hydrogen-based steel production, molten oxide electrolysis, and process improvements that reduce coke use. It also notes major investments in commercial green steel plants and startups working toward CO2-free steel production.

title: Low-Carbon Production of Iron & Steel: Technology Options and Economic Assessment
source: Center on Global Energy Policy, Columbia University
summary: This report reviews current iron and steel production and evaluates decarbonization pathways for the sector. It identifies hydrogen, biomass, zero-carbon electricity, and carbon capture and storage as promising options for reducing emissions from steelmaking.

title: Op-ed: navigating the steel industry's global transformation through technology and collaboration
summary: The piece discusses how technology is reshaping the steel industry, with a focus on AI, electric arc furnaces, and hydrogen in the context of climate targets and market pressures. It emphasizes collaboration and operational visibility as key to building a more resilient and sustainable steel sector.

title: Copper futures slip below $6.25 as trade optimism fades
summary: Copper futures fell below $6.25 per pound after briefly reaching a one-week high of $6.40 earlier in the week. The move was attributed to cautious optimism around trade developments, with prices easing as market sentiment cooled.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows copper futures trading at 6.2985, slightly below the previous close of 6.3383. The page provides live pricing and daily range data, which can be used to track short-term market movement and volatility.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel has been told to drastically cut output, with its ore production quota for this year reportedly reduced to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025. The report says Indonesia is trying to support global nickel prices by limiting mine output, and that the company plans to request a revision.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: This piece argues that nickel fundamentals are improving as Indonesia keeps production flat in 2026 while EV demand continues to grow. It says supply discipline and grade constraints could support prices toward $20,000 per ton.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia planned to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article says the move also reflects a shift toward tighter regulation of mining quotas and environmentally harmful operations.

title: Global Nickel Market
summary: This market update says global mine nickel supply rose from 3.6 million metric tons in 2023 to 3.9 million in 2024. It also notes that the market surplus remains relatively modest, which can matter for price and capacity trends.

title: Nickel Processing Grows with EV Demand, Production Efforts
summary: This article focuses on how nickel processing capacity is expanding alongside EV-related demand. It describes production efforts tied to the broader growth in nickel use for stainless steel, specialty alloys, and batteries.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The report says PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been told to sharply cut output, with a proposed quota reduction from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year. The article frames the move as part of Indonesia's effort to support global nickel prices, which directly affects the supply chain for battery and clean-energy metals.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, the stainless steel scrap business of Italian metal recycler Com.Steel. The deal was described as an upstream integration move, and it was expected to close in the coming months.
